title: Module 6 handout. One project with 6 blocks.
subtitle: Project stages, data management, reflection – print and use at work.
projectSteps: Goal and role (META) – what you create, for whom, what role. You can pause and save.
Sources and constraints (INPUT + RAG) – what data you use, RAG guidelines.
Research plan (REASONING / Deep research) – steps, sub-questions.
Quality and checks (QUALITY) – sources, "I don't know", objectivity.
Technical settings (ADVANCED) – temperature, max_tokens. When ready: run the prompt and get the report.

dataManagementPoints.title: 1. Personal prompt library
dataManagementPoints.practicalMeaning: Keep all "working" prompts in one place (Notion, file, tool) – no need to dig through old chats.

dataManagementPoints.title: 2. Prompt versioning
dataManagementPoints.practicalMeaning: When you change a prompt – save a copy with date or v1, v2; later you'll see what worked better.

dataManagementPoints.title: 3. Documentation
dataManagementPoints.practicalMeaning: 2–3 sentences per template: what it's for, what to paste, what to expect – in a month you or a colleague will know how to use it.

dataManagementPoints.title: 4. Processes
dataManagementPoints.practicalMeaning: One document or diagram "how we do X with AI" – new joiners or you after a break won't need to reverse-engineer.

dataManagementPoints.title: 5. Data sets
dataManagementPoints.practicalMeaning: 5–10 line sample (e.g. customer feedback) – you change the prompt and in 1 min check if the result is correct.

reflectionSummary: Reflection: (1) What was hardest in the project? (2) Which skill will you use first at work? (3) What else do you want to learn about AI? First action within 24–48h: open your AI tool and add one prompt from the module to your library or use the 6-block structure for another topic.
footerText: Prompt anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
